key_insight: The paper replaces algebraic residual fitting in fixed-pattern sparse approximate inverse preconditioners with spectral optimization of the congruence-transformed operator to cluster eigenvalues near ±1 and improve solver behavior.
engineering_relevance: This can strengthen iterative finite-element solvers for large symmetric positive definite and especially symmetric indefinite saddle-point systems, where robust preconditioning is critical for scalable mechanics simulations.
method: Choose admissible entries of a fixed sparse factor G by minimizing spectral objectives of P(G)=GAG^T
Use Lanczos-based projected Krylov support-gradients with a detached Rayleigh surrogate to obtain matrix-free gradients on the prescribed sparsity pattern
For indefinite systems, add a bimodal loss that separates positive and negative eigenvalues while penalizing near-zero modes
evidence_signal: Experiments on finite-element test problems show improved fixed-support preconditioners, with particularly strong gains for symmetric indefinite saddle-point systems, and the authors also demonstrate a graph neural network predictor for transferable factor entries.
watch_item: The approach depends on Lanczos/Krylov approximations and a fixed sparsity pattern, so performance may be sensitive to spectral estimation quality and pattern choice.
paper_title: Factored Sparse Approximate Inverse Preconditioning via Spectral Optimization
